title: Uromorus anthopophagorum
taxonomy: Uromorus anthopophagorum is classified as a species of freshwater snail, with more specific classification details being Uromorus anthopophagorum being part of the family Lymnaeidae. This classification makes it a type of lymnaeid freshwater snail. Uromorus anthopophagorum is found in Africa, specifically in the rivers and streams of countries such as South Africa. It belongs to the kingdom Animalia, phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, and order Pulmonata. Further classification includes the family Lymnaeidae, genus Uromorus, and species U. anthopophagorum.

constituents: Uromorus anthopophagorum, also known as the African walnut, contains various active constituents that have been studied for their medicinal properties. These active constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The flavonoids present in Uromorus anthopophagorum have been shown to exhibit hepatoprotective and cardioprotective effects.
